cot_content:
Okay, so I need to figure out how to tell the right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are either tying a knot at the speaker end of the left one or at the connecting end of the left one.

First, let me visualize earbuds. Each earbud has two parts: the speaker part that goes into the ear and the connecting wire that links to the other earbud. The idea is to mark the left earbud in some way so you can tell which is which by touch.

Option A says to tie a knot at the speaker end of the left earbud. That means the part that goes into your left ear would have a knot. So when you put them in, you'd feel the knot on the left side.

Option B suggests tying a knot at the connecting end of the left earbud. The connecting end is where the wire joins the other earbud. If you tie a knot there, it might be harder to feel when inserting the earbuds because that part is not in your ear. Also, the connecting ends are usually on the same side when you wear them, so you might not be able to distinguish left from right by touch there.

Tying a knot at the speaker end (Option A) makes more sense because when you insert the earbuds, you can feel the knot in your left ear, helping you identify which one is left. The knot at the connecting end (Option B) might not be as useful because you don't feel that part when wearing them. Plus, the connecting ends are on the outside, so you might not be able to tell which side the knot is on by touch when inserting the earbuds.

So the correct answer is likely Option A.
label: majority
